content.problemStatement:
What do we observe here / what is the logic?
- Whenever $N \leq M$ - i.e. the number of pair of shoes required is less than the number of left shoes available, then Chef needs to buy $N$ right shoes
- When $N \gt M$ - how many shoes does Chef need to buy?
  - Chef already has $M$ left shoes. He will need to buy $(N - M)$ left shoes
  - Chef will need to buy $N$ right shoes
  - Chef will need to buy a total of $(N + N - M)$, i.e. $(2*N - M)$ shoes 

Once the observation / logic is clear, click on 'Next' to proceed.
content.inputFormat:
- The first line contains a single integer $T$ - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
- The first line of each test case contains two integers $N$ and $M$ - the number of Chef's friends and the number of left shoes Chef has.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output the minimum number of extra shoes that Chef will have to buy to ensure that he is able to get $N$ pairs of shoes.
